paraphrase.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda
paraphrase.body: Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda, starting 15 July. The move expands the airline's network to over 160 destinations. According to the airline, four weekly flights to Helsinki will increase to daily service from 1 August, while Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights from 15 July. HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani praised the expansion, highlighting its significance for Qatar's connectivity.
key_facts: Flights resume to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from 15 July.
Helsinki: four weekly flights, increasing to daily from 1 August.
Tokyo Haneda: four weekly flights from 15 July.

gi_applied.what: Normalized 'His Highness the Amir' to 'HH Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani'.
Added local-impact angle: connectivity for Qatar.
Opened with varied lead: 'Qatar Airways has announced...'.
